macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stock Markets Surge as Strait of Hormuz Reopens, Oil Prices Plummet
items.summary: Global stock markets rallied following news that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen after a prolonged closure due to Iran conflict. The S&P 500 climbed 1.3% to a new record, while oil prices dropped approximately 14% to $81 per barrel, down from a peak of $112 earlier in the month.

items.title: Federal Reserve Governor Warns Markets May Undervalue Conflict Risks
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Waller cautioned that oil futures prices and securities markets may be undervaluing the risk of continued conflict and prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure. He noted that March inflation data showed energy prices surging 10.8% in one month, pushing headline inflation to 3.3% and core inflation to 2.6%.

items.title: Trump Administration Implements Naval Blockade to Ease Iran Conflict
items.summary: The Trump administration implemented a U.S. naval blockade mirroring Iran's toll-based blockade on ships, which appears to have been effective in de-escalating tensions. President Trump stated the blockade will remain in effect until transactions with Iran are 100% complete.

items.title: Global Energy and Commodity Prices Spike from Middle East Disruptions
items.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz caused widespread price increases for gasoline, jet fuel, natural gas, fertilizer, and helium globally, with Europe and Asia experiencing particularly severe impacts. Some regions have already been forced to implement rationing measures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits Two-Month High of $77,400 Amid Strait of Hormuz Reopening
items.summary: Bitcoin surged 5% to a two-month high of around $77,400, with Ethereum climbing to $2,464, driven by Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz easing oil concerns and boosting crypto stocks like Coinbase and Robinhood.[1][2][6]

items.title: Kraken Parent Payward Acquires Bitnomial for Up to $550 Million
items.summary: Payward, Kraken's parent, agreed to buy derivatives exchange Bitnomial for up to $550 million in a cash-and-stock deal valuing Payward at $20 billion, gaining access to advanced crypto settlement infrastructure.[1]

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Reach $812 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: Total network liquidations hit $812 million over the past 24 hours, with shorts liquidated at $648 million and longs at $163 million, amid significant market volatility.[3][4]

items.title: XRP Surges Past $1.49 with ETF Funds Exceeding $1 Billion
items.summary: XRP rose up to 4.51% to $1.49, fueled by integration into Solana’s DeFi, ETF funds surpassing $1 billion, and increased trading volume to $4.52 billion.[1][5]

items.title: Ethereum Faces Heavy Shorting and $111.6 Million Liquidations
items.summary: Ethereum gained 6.36% to $2,464 despite $111.6 million in 24-hour liquidations, mostly longs, amid year-to-date weakness but a structural breakout.[5][6]

items.title: Polygon Launches sPOL Liquid Staking Token Unlocking $330 Million
items.summary: Polygon introduced sPOL, a liquid staking token that unlocks approximately $330 million in staked capital, enhancing liquidity dynamics.[5]

items.title: Circle Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over $232 Million USDC Theft
items.summary: Stablecoin issuer Circle is sued by Drift Protocol investors for allegedly failing to freeze $232 million in stolen USDC from an April 1 exploit.[1]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: ChatGPT 5.3 Reduces Outbound Links with Stricter Authority Signals
items.summary: OpenAI's ChatGPT 5.3 expands query searches to 10+ per prompt but cites 20% fewer sources, prioritizing credibility via authority signals like credentials and awards. This continues the trend of declining referral traffic to publishers.

items.title: Anthropic Prepares Claude Opus 4.7 with Full-Stack AI Studio Tools
items.summary: Anthropic advances toward a full-stack AI studio by releasing Claude Opus 4.7 alongside a design tool for generating websites, presentations, and landing pages from natural language. Opus 4.7 reserves Mythos as a restricted frontier model.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind for Life Sciences Research
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Rosalind, a new reasoning model specialized for biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine research.

items.title: Google Releases Standalone Gemini App for macOS
items.summary: Google launched a Gemini app for Mac with a floating interface, contextual on-screen queries, media generation, and Google Drive integration to compete in desktop AI workflows.

items.title: Anthropic Faces Backlash Over Claude Performance Decline
items.summary: Users report reduced reasoning depth and accuracy in Claude, attributed by Anthropic to settings, amid speculation of compute constraints or prioritization of models like Mythos, highlighting AI access fragmentation.

items.title: Alibaba Releases Qwen3.6-35B-A3B Model
items.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team released Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, a new AI model highlighted in recent AI updates.

items.title: Microsoft Unveils MAI-Image-2-Efficient
items.summary: Microsoft announced MAI-Image-2-Efficient, an efficient image generation model.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Releases Hotfix for Starfield PS5 Crashes Amid Refund Demands
items.summary: Bethesda acknowledged and released a hotfix for widespread bugs and glitches making Starfield unplayable on PS5, about a week after launch, as players demand refunds while Xbox player count rises nearly 200%.

items.title: Metro 2039 Fully Revealed with Winter 2026 Release
items.summary: 4A Games unveiled Metro 2039 with a cinematic trailer showing gameplay footage, set for release this winter, noting the game was fundamentally changed by Russia's illegal war.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake Reveal Imminent
items.summary: Leaks indicate the Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake is set to be officially revealed soon, according to Insider Gaming reports.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Wins Best Game at 2026 BAFTA Awards
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 took the BAFTA for Best Game, with Dispatch leading other winners announced on April 17, 2026.

items.title: Hades 2 Launches on PS5 and Xbox with Romance Update
items.summary: Hades 2 arrives on PS5 and Xbox alongside a major update simplifying romance with a popular character, available now for all versions.

items.title: SNK Announces Modern NeoGeo AES Console with Original Game Compatibility
items.summary: SNK revealed a new NeoGeo AES console offering unrivaled authenticity, fully compatible with original games and featuring £70 cartridges.

items.title: Starfield Rated for Nintendo Switch 2, Confirming Imminent Port
items.summary: Bethesda's Starfield RPG has received a rating for Nintendo Switch 2, strongly indicating a port release soon.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to scale the mobile OS to desktops and reshape personal computing.

items.title: US Senator Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's Acquisition of SchedMD
items.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren has expressed national security and competition concerns regarding Nvidia's acquisition of SchedMD, which controls Slurm software used in US government supercomputers.

items.title: India Smartphone Shipments Hit 6-Year Low in Q1 2026
items.summary: India's smartphone shipments fell to a six-year low in the first quarter of 2026 due to price hikes, according to Counterpoint Research.

items.title: Sequoia Leaders Raise $7 Billion for Major Investments
items.summary: Sequoia Capital's new leaders have raised approximately $7 billion for the firm's largest investment bets amid ongoing tech funding activity.

items.title: Wipro Attrition Drops to 13.8% with Slight Headcount Increase
items.summary: Wipro reported its attrition rate falling to 13.8% while headcount grew by 136 employees, signaling stabilization in the IT services sector.
